title: ellertonia
intro: Ellertonia is a genus of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the wild quinine or snake root. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and snake bites. Its roots and leaves are rich in alkaloids, such as ellertin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, providing relief from pain, swelling, and infections. Ellertonia can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, offering a natural remedy for various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
